questions.question: What is the primary purpose of Notion Academy as described in the article?

options.text: To sell third‑party Notion templates on a marketplace.
options.explanation: Selling templates is mentioned as a lesson topic, not the overall purpose of the Academy.

options.text: To help users turn curiosity into capability by learning Notion and earning badges.
options.explanation: The opening tagline states that Notion Academy turns curiosity into capability, offering learning paths and badges.

options.text: To provide a place for users to chat in real time about Notion features.
options.explanation: Live chat isn’t mentioned; the article focuses on structured lessons, webinars, and community links.

options.text: To replace the Notion Help Center as the sole source of documentation.
options.explanation: The Help Center is still listed as a separate resource for quick answers.

questions.question: Which statement best describes the “Notion Certified Admin” badge?

options.text: It is only available to users on the Enterprise plan.
options.explanation: The article says the certification is available for any plan type, not just Enterprise.

options.text: It recognizes individuals who can run Notion at scale, implement security best practices, and design smarter workspaces.
options.explanation: The description of the Certified Admin badge matches this answer.

options.text: It is automatically granted after completing any three lessons.
options.explanation: Badges are earned by completing curated paths, not by a generic three‑lesson rule.

options.text: It focuses solely on advanced database formulas.
options.explanation: While advanced topics may be covered, the badge is broader, covering admin and security skills.

questions.question: If a beginner wants to learn the fundamentals of navigating Notion, which lesson should they start with?

options.text: Building basics
options.explanation: The “Building basics” lesson is labeled Beginner and covers navigation, blocks, databases, and wikis.

options.text: An issue tracker for any team
options.explanation: That lesson is Intermediate and focuses on building an intake system, not basic navigation.

options.text: Workspace design
options.explanation: Workspace design is an Advanced lesson about best‑practice design, not a beginner overview.

options.text: Templates & marketplace
options.explanation: This lesson targets intermediate users interested in creating and selling templates.

questions.question: Which learning path would be most appropriate for someone who already knows the basics and wants to automate work using AI?

options.text: Essentials Path
options.explanation: Essentials Path is aimed at beginners new to Notion.

options.text: Workflows Path
options.explanation: The Workflows Path is labeled Intermediate and mentions creating connected systems with AI.

options.text: Advanced Path
options.explanation: Advanced Path focuses on deeper automation and architecture, but the question specifies “using AI” which is highlighted in the Workflows Path description.

options.text: Custom Agents lesson
options.explanation: While Custom Agents teach automation, it’s a single lesson, not a structured path.

questions.question: How does the article suggest users can get quick answers to detailed Notion questions?

options.text: By attending a live webinar.
options.explanation: Webinars are for skill development, not specifically for quick answers.

options.text: By visiting the Help Center.
options.explanation: The “Still learning?” section points to the Help Center for quick answers.

options.text: By joining the community forum.
options.explanation: Community is for networking and inspiration, not the primary source for quick answers.

options.text: By reading the badge descriptions.
options.explanation: Badge descriptions provide motivation, not detailed troubleshooting.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about “Custom Agents” is true?

options.text: Custom Agents can only run on a fixed schedule and cannot respond to triggers.
options.explanation: The article says they can run on a schedule *or* respond to triggers.

options.text: Custom Agents are designed to automate repeat work and can integrate with tools you already use.
options.explanation: This matches the description: automate repeat work, run on schedule or triggers, work with existing tools.

options.text: Custom Agents are exclusive to the Advanced Path and require a premium subscription.
options.explanation: The lesson is labeled Beginner and no premium requirement is mentioned.

options.text: Custom Agents replace the need for any human involvement in Notion workflows.
options.explanation: They reduce busywork but do not fully replace human decision‑making.

questions.question: What is the relationship between “Badged Paths” and the lessons listed under them?

options.text: Each Badged Path contains a fixed number of lessons that, when completed, award a badge.
options.explanation: The article lists paths (Essentials, Workflows, Advanced) with a set number of lessons and describes them as “Badged Path.”

options.text: Badged Paths are optional side‑tracks that do not affect badge eligibility.
options.explanation: The term “Badged Path” implies completion leads to a badge, not that they’re optional.

options.text: Badged Paths are only available after you earn the Notion Certified Admin badge.
options.explanation: There is no prerequisite stated; paths are open to any learner.

options.text: Badged Paths are exclusively for advanced users and contain only AI‑focused lessons.
options.explanation: Paths range from Beginner (Essentials) to Advanced, and not all are AI‑focused.

questions.question: Which resource would you use if you wanted to both learn a new skill and earn a certification that is recognized by your employer?

options.text: Live webinars
options.explanation: Webinars provide learning but do not directly grant a recognized certification.

options.text: Notion Certified Admin program
options.explanation: The Certified Admin program offers a formal certification that is recognized and can be showcased to employers.

options.text: Community forums
options.explanation: Forums are great for networking but do not issue official certifications.

options.text: Badge‑earning Badged Paths
options.explanation: Badged Paths award internal Notion badges, not an external certification recognized by employers.
